commands.repair_reason: - [P1] A maintainer should obtain current-head redacted transport proof and confirm the deliberate shared-provider compatibility contract; there is no concrete automated code repair to make. Likely related people - stei...; Cleared: The patch reduces credential redirect exposure without adding dependencies, executable supply-chain inputs, broader permissions, secret logging, or new credential storage. (sha=79865f99420d6478168a86b92f534def149bd21f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared same-origin HTTPS redirect guard for provider HTTP traffic, routes three cookie-bearing OpenAI dashboard request paths through it, and adds focused redirect-policy tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main uses unguarded production URLSession redirect handling for the shared client, and three cookie-bearing OpenAI paths use URLSession.shared directly. The submitted smoke also demonstrates the original redirect weakness and intended rejection cases without using real accounts.
commands.review_followup:
- [P1] A maintainer should obtain current-head redacted transport proof and confirm the deliberate shared-provider compatibility contract; there is no concrete automated code repair to make.

Likely related people
- `steipete` — high confidence; authored the force-pushed final policy and owns the available current-main history for the central files.
- `Hinotoi-agent` — high confidence; developed the prior security iterations, regression tests, and runtime proof.

target.title: [security] fix(providers): guard credentialed redirects
target.body:
## Summary

Harden provider HTTP redirects at the shared transport boundary.

- Use a redirect-guarded production session for both `ProviderHTTPClient.shared` and default-created clients.
- Reject redirects when the original URL is unavailable.
- Reject HTTP downgrades, cross-host redirects, and port changes.
- Preserve same-origin HTTPS redirects unchanged so authenticated provider flows keep working.
- Route ChatGPT dashboard usage, identity, and cookie-import identity requests through the guarded provider client.

## Security boundary

Provider requests can carry browser cookies, bearer tokens, or API keys. Before this change, the shared production client used URLSession's default redirect handling, and several cookie-bearing OpenAI dashboard requests bypassed the shared client entirely.

Redirects now remain within the initial HTTPS scheme, host, and normalized port. A request is never replayed to a different origin through this transport. Provider-specific redirect delegates remain responsible for their explicitly custom redirect policies.

## Compatibility

Same-origin HTTPS redirects continue with the request produced by URLSession, including credentials that are valid for that origin. Cross-origin redirects now fail instead of following automatically. Provider API endpoints and custom endpoint overrides should return their final credentialed endpoint directly.

## Validation

- `swift test --filter ProviderHTTPClientTests` - 13 tests passed.
- `make check` - SwiftFormat and strict SwiftLint passed.
- Local autoreview - clean, confidence 0.94.
- Full branch autoreview against `origin/main` - clean, confidence 0.90.

No live provider credentials or browser sessions were used.
target.author: hinotoi-agent

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
